summary: Gruber follows up on his earlier analysis of Mac OS X Panther adoption rates among Daring Fireball's Safari-using readers. Tracking daily server logs from Panther's October 23 release through the end of November, he shows adoption climbing from 16% on launch day to nearly 80% within five weeks. He also shares full browser usage stats for November, revealing Safari as the top browser among his readers, with IE Windows close behind and Mozilla/Firebird variants making a notable showing. The data paints a picture of an enthusiastic, early-adopter Mac audience upgrading at a remarkable pace.
key_points: Panther adoption among Safari users went from 16% on launch day to ~79% within five weeks, showing extraordinarily fast OS adoption
By the end of the first week, Panther had already reached 50% of Safari users — half the audience upgraded within days
Safari was the single most-used browser on Daring Fireball with 18,382 unique IPs, narrowly beating IE Windows at 15,237
The Mozilla family (Camino, Firebird, Mozilla) collectively represented a significant alternative browser presence alongside Safari
The data is explicitly limited to Safari users, making it a measure of enthusiasm among Mac-focused early adopters rather than the general population
money_quotes: Remember, these figures are *only* Safari users. The Percent column indicates the percentage of Safari users who are running Panther.
While I'm at it, here are the Daring Fireball browser usage stats for the month of November.
key_insight: Mac OS X Panther was adopted at a strikingly rapid pace, with nearly 80% of Daring Fireball's Safari audience upgrading within just five weeks of release, reflecting the enthusiasm of Apple's core user base.
